toc_content:
Requirements on SOME/IP Protocol
AUTOSAR FO Release 1.5.0
Table of Contents
1 Scope of this document 6
1.1 Document Conventions : 7
2 Acronyms and Abbreviations 8
3 Requirements Tracing 9
4 Requirements 10
4.1 General : 10
[RS_SOMEIP_00002] SOME/IP protocol shall provide service-based
communication : 10
[RS_SOMEIP_00041] SOME/IP protocol shall provide support of multi-
ple versions of the protocol : 10
[RS_SOMEIP_00003] SOME/IP protocol shall provide support of multi-
ple versions of a service interface : 10
[RS_SOMEIP_00004] SOME/IP protocol shall support event communi-
cation : 11
[RS_SOMEIP_00005] SOME/IP protocol shall support different strate-
gies for event communication : 11
[RS_SOMEIP_00042] SOME/IP protocol shall support unicast and mul-
ticast based event communication : 12
[RS_SOMEIP_00006] SOME/IP protocol shall support uni-directional
RPC communication : 12
[RS_SOMEIP_00007] SOME/IP protocol shall support bi-directional
RPC communication : 12
[RS_SOMEIP_00008] SOME/IP protocol shall support error handling of
RPC communication : 13
[RS_SOMEIP_00009] SOME/IP protocol shall support field communica-
tion : 13
[RS_SOMEIP_00010] SOME/IP protocol shall support different trans-
port protocols underneath : 14
[RS_SOMEIP_00011] SOME/IP protocol shall support messages of dif-
ferent lengths : 14
[RS_SOMEIP_00012] SOME/IP protocol shall support session handling 14
[RS_SOMEIP_00014] SOME/IP protocol shall support handling of pro-
tocol errors on receiver side : 15
[RS_SOMEIP_00051] SOME/IP protocol shall provide support for seg-
mented transmission of large data : 15
4.2 Services : 16
[RS_SOMEIP_00019] SOME/IP protocol shall identify services using
unique identifiers : 16
[RS_SOMEIP_00015] SOME/IP protocol shall support multiple in-
stances of a service : 16
3 of 27
— AUTOSAR CONFIDENTIAL —Document ID 800: AUTOSAR_RS_SOMEIPProtocol
